mnemonics.title: & is for Address
mnemonics.content: Remember that the ampersand `&` looks like a little 'a' which can stand for 'address'. So `&variable` gives you the 'address' of the variable.

mnemonics.title: * is for Value (at the target)
mnemonics.content: The asterisk `*` looks like a star you aim at. You 'aim' the pointer at an address, and the `*` operator lets you get the 'value' at that target.

mnemonics.title: MALLOC: Memory ALLOCation
mnemonics.content: The function name itself is the mnemonic: `malloc` is short for Memory ALLOCation.

mnemonics.title: Always FREE your MALLOC
mnemonics.content: This rhymes and emphasizes the critical rule: for every `malloc` (or `calloc`), there must be a corresponding `free` to prevent memory leaks.
